transcript.text:
(Pyeongyang naengmyeon* Part 1) *Cold buckwheat noodles
('Eulmildae', Mapo-gu, Seoul)
(220405, cleared)
(Pyeongyang naengmyeon Part 2)
('Jinmi Pyongyang Naengmyeon', Gangnam-gu, Seoul)
(220808, cleared)
(And there was a notice for part 3...) Which place should I try next?
(But there was no news on it for a year...)
I'm here to shoot the Pyeongyang naengmyeon series part 3
(Thank you for your patience)
(Pyeongyang naengmyeon part 3, 'Jeongin Myeonok')
(Let's get it) Yum
(The dignity of the three major Pyeongyang naengmyeon restaurants)
(The coldness of the Pyeongyang naengmyeon will cool down the heat)
Among all the Pyeongyang naengmyeons I tried so far,
I think this has been the highest level

(So this time... I'll explain about the taste) It's like... There's a strong....
(This time, I'll get to the bottom of this...!) It's hard to explain
Hello everyone
Today,
I'm at Yeouido
I came to shoot Pyeongyang naengmyeon series part 3
I tried Pyeongyang naengmyeon for the first time...
(Tried it last year in April & August) Last year? And this is my third time
(It tastes strange, but addictive..lol) It tastes strange, but I've been craving it
This is...
(Restaurant 'Jeongin Myeonok') This place has a 50-year-old tradition
It received Blue Ribbon and Michelin Awards every year
Apparently this is an amazing must-visit place
During lunch,
I heard you have to wait for very long
so I came at dinner time, just after their break time

Anyway, I'll head in
(As soon as I walked in,)
(it was full of traces proving that it is a must-visit place)
Mhm
Yum
A hind shank suyuk, dumplings, and mung bean jeon are a must
(Everything is a must here) Noodles are a must, & a spicy one too
(Today's order) Can I get...
Two cold buckwheat noodles, one spicy buckwheat noodles
And a hind shank suyuk* *Boiled beef slices
and dumplings, and mung bean jeon* *Korean-style savory pancakes
- OKay! - And...
- No cucumbers, please - For all of them?
Yes! Thank you
(Sweet and savory spicy naengmyeon)
(Cold noodles with broth, the root of Pyongyang naengmyeon)

(Mung bean jeon, which goes well with cold noodles)
(After eating the noodles, take a big bite of its friends, the dumplings...)
(And hind shank suyuk, a must-try dish at this restaurant)
(The noodles are here)
Thank you!
Thank you
- Please enjoy - Okay!
(A table full of noodles & other dishes) Wow, Pyeongyang naengmyeon! Yum!
Which one should I try first?
I should try the noodles with broth first, right?
(The broth of Pyeongyang naengmyeon is clear and clean)
The amount of noodles will serve two people!
I'll have to keep the noodles disentangled
(Generous amount of noodles that fill up the bowl)
Why are there so many noodles?
(The portion for 1 has a very generous amount...)

Thank you for the food
I'll try the noodles with broth first
I haven't had Pyeongyang naengmyeon in such a long time!
How long has it been? I ate it a few months ago, right?
(The last time was last August, so 10 months ago...!)
Oh, this bland taste...!
Oh, food here is good
It's slightly bland
and it's slightly savory!
There's a deep....
First of all,
the noodles aren't chewy
They're very soft noodles
(It's hard to describe the taste yet...!) It's hard to explain the texture
But compared to the other noodles,
these noodles aren't chewy

They are definitely choppy
Since the broth is very bland,
I didn't think the noodles would've soaked up the broth
Or is this the unique taste of these noodles?
I want to eat the mung bean jeon
Wow!
(It's crispy on the outside & soft inside) It's crispy on the outside, it's amazing
It's very, very crispy
I'll dip it in here
It's good
The mung bean jeon is a must!
(It goes very well with the noodles) The hind shank suyuk
The meat pieces aren't warm
It's slightly cold!
It's a cold kind of suyuk
But I love the texture

(It's not too heavy, so it's perfect) This is perfect to have for lunch
I understand why there are so many customers at lunch
There's no smell of meat inside, and it's tasty
This doesn't smell at all either
It's very...
I really like the texture
(A plate of big dumplings...!) The dumplings are really big
I think they were hand-made
(The dumplings are filled up to the top...) It's filled up with the fillings inside
It's full of fillings inside
This also tastes like...
The North Korean dumplings!
Should I try the spicy noodles?
It's very flavorful!
(With a piece of suyuk on top...)
Seasoning!

(The seasoning is spicy & sour) I'll add plenty, the seasoning is tasty
Oh, it tastes delicious with lots of seasoning
I should've ordered two of the spicy noodles...
From this hind shank suyuk....
I do like the meat part too
But you know this tendon part?
(Falls) I...
I really like that part
It's chewy
(A flood)
(The shirt always eats together...) Even when I'm eating white food...
I think the unique taste is different for each restaurant
'Eulmildae' is for beginners
'Jinmi' and 'Jeongin Myeonok' are pretty similar, at intermediate level?)
But this is a huge portion
(This is amazing)

(Cough)
On this,
(The jeon goes well with pickled onions) I'll add onions
I'll wrap this in kimchi
When you eat Pyeongyang naengmyeon,
I think it tastes better
when you eat the noodles with the broth
I think,
Among all the Pyeongyang naengmyeons I tried so far,
(Pyeongyang naengmyeon level. MAX) I think this has been the highest level
When you eat this from restaurants of lower levels,
the flavors are strong
But the higher the level,
(The noodles taste even more bland) the flavors become more bland
(It's confusing, but you'll crave it...) You'd think 'What flavor is this?'
Is there a way to eat this that I haven't tried before?

Should I add a bit of vinegar?
But those who really love these noodles wouldn't!
- Only beginners would add it in! - (Upset) Did you call me a beginner?
(Ended up being a beginner...) I'll just be a beginner
(Adding the teardrops of a beginner...)
(I should've listened to the expert...) No, it'd be better without it
I'm glad I added it in here
It doesn't go well with it
(Adding the runny nose of a beginner) It doesn't go well together!
You're supposed to eat that with the radish!
(Realized the best way to suyuk late) It tastes better like this!
(It's hard to become an expert) Not an expert of it yet
It really doesn't taste heavy at all
It's a party of blandness
The good thing about not tasting heavy
is that it doesn't leave an unpleasant taste, and you don't get sick of it

The cold noodles with broth became very mushy
so I'll add a bit of this!
(Drip-)
I'm Pyeongyang naengmyeons don't easily get mushy
I think that's a relief
If this was a different type of noodle, it would've been mushy by now, right?
The noodles haven't become mushy at all!
(It's spicy)
You have to add these onions to the mung bean jeon
That makes it taste delicious
(Young radish kimchi) What's this?
The young radish kimchi
would go well with the mung bean jeon
(The jeon stayed crispy until the end) Look at how it has stayed crispy
But above all,
there's nothing quite like the pickled onions

This
doesn't taste good because of the vinegar
(Moving to the broth that tastes good)
I don't really like the eggs
in cold noodles
They seem like they're not seasoned!
But the eggs in spicy noodles
tastes good
Pyeongyang naengmyeons kind of taste like milk!
Like...
(I still don't know about you...) It's hard to explain
Lots of customers started coming in now
And I happened to have finished eating!
(Finished eating Pyeongyang naengmyeon)
Wow, among all the Pyeongyang naengmyeons I've tried so far,
I think this was one of the high-level ones

(It's not heavy & I keep craving it) As you eat it, it gets addictive!
(Wiped down the bowls quietly...) I ate them all without talking
(Pyeongyang naengmyeons experts!) If you like Pyeongyang naengmyeon,
(Please recommend a place for part 4) please recommend a place for me
Thank you
for another great meal today!
